source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, isosceles right triangles are constructed externally on each side of $\triangle ABC$ using the sides of $\triangle ABC$ as the right angles. If $AB = \sqrt{3}$, what is the area of the shaded region in the figure?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution:} Since $ABC$ is a right-angled triangle,\\
we have $AC^{2}+BC^{2}=AB^{2}=3$,\\
thus, the area of the shaded region $S_{\text{shaded}}= \frac{1}{2} AC^{2}+ \frac{1}{2} BC^{2}+ \frac{1}{2} AB^{2}= \frac{1}{2} (AC^{2}+BC^{2})+ \frac{1}{2} AB^{2}= \frac{1}{2} AB^{2}+ \frac{1}{2} AB^{2}=AB^{2}=\boxed{3}.$
